name: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights (Banjul Charter 1981)
summary: The African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights (popularly the Banjul Charter) is a Sub-Saharan African supranational meta-framework human-rights binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Organisation of African Unity (OAU — succeeded by the African Union AU) — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating formal combined individual + collective peoples' rights framework (African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted 27 June 1981 + effective 21 October 1986 — formal African regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework including formal Article 19-24 peoples' rights framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate on individual-rights framework without formal peoples'-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional human-rights binding-treaty framework operating formal peoples'-rights framework), as the third modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework (Banjul Charter + African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights established via Protocol of 10 June 1998 effective 25 January 2004 + African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights established 1987 — formal African regional court framework + dedicated commission framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate without formal regional-court framework, structurally distinctive globally as one of three modern regional human-rights frameworks with formal regional-court framework alongside ECHR + IACtHR), as the central jurisdiction of the formal African Article 18 family-rights + Article 19-24 peoples'-rights framework (Banjul Charter Article 18 framework on Family + Articles 19-24 peoples'-rights framework — formal foundational family-rights + peoples'-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional human-rights binding-treaty framework operating formal combined family-rights + peoples'-rights framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child ACRWC 1990 complementary framework (Banjul Charter Article 18 framework + ACRWC 1990/1999 framework operating complementary children's-rights + family-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating formal complementary children's-rights binding-treaty framework). The Banjul Charter is binding upon 55 of 55 African Union member states + 1 of 2 Western Saharan Arab Democratic Republic SADR state party framework — universal African coverage framework. The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 18 family-rights framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework. The African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights + African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights are the apex judicial + monitoring bodies. The Banjul Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights 1981 (Banjul Charter)
statutory_framework.title: Banjul Charter
statutory_framework.relevance: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted 27 June 1981 in Nairobi by OAU + effective 21 October 1986 — formal African regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework.

statutory_framework.title: Banjul Charter Article 18 + Articles 19-24
statutory_framework.relevance: Banjul Charter Article 18 framework on Family + Articles 19-24 peoples'-rights framework — formal foundational family-rights + peoples'-rights framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Protocol Establishing the African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights 1998
statutory_framework.title: African Court Protocol 1998
statutory_framework.relevance: Protocol Establishing the African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted 10 June 1998 + effective 25 January 2004 — formal African regional court framework.

statutory_framework.citation: African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child 1990 (ACRWC)
statutory_framework.title: African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child
statutory_framework.relevance: African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child adopted 11 July 1990 + effective 29 November 1999 — formal complementary children's-rights binding-treaty framework, framework subordinate to Banjul Charter framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Maputo Protocol 2003 (African Charter on Rights of Women)
statutory_framework.title: Maputo Protocol
statutory_framework.relevance: Protocol to the African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights on the Rights of Women in Africa adopted 11 July 2003 in Maputo + effective 25 November 2005 — formal Banjul Charter Protocol on women's rights framework.

apex_courts.name: African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body framework for Banjul Charter + ACRWC + Maputo Protocol.

apex_courts.name: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
apex_courts.role: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights — formal African human rights monitoring body, established 1987.

anonymisation_convention: Banjul Charter-related individual communications are anonymised per African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights practice using initials.

key_developments.title: OAU Charter
key_developments.description: Organisation of African Unity Charter signed 25 May 1963 — beginning of formal African regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: Banjul Charter adopted
key_developments.description: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted 27 June 1981 by OAU XVIII Assembly of Heads of State and Government in Nairobi — formal African regional binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: Banjul Charter enters force
key_developments.description: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights entered force 21 October 1986 — formal African regional binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights established
key_developments.description: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights established 2 November 1987 — formal African human rights monitoring body.

key_developments.title: ACRWC adopted
key_developments.description: African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child ACRWC adopted 11 July 1990 — formal complementary children's-rights binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: African Court Protocol adopted
key_developments.description: Protocol Establishing the African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights adopted 10 June 1998 — formal African regional court framework.

key_developments.title: African Union established
key_developments.description: African Union established 9 July 2002 — succession from Organisation of African Unity OAU framework.

key_developments.title: African Court Protocol enters force
key_developments.description: Protocol Establishing the African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights entered force 25 January 2004 — formal African regional court framework operational.

key_developments.title: Maputo Protocol enters force
key_developments.description: Protocol to the African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights on the Rights of Women in Africa Maputo Protocol entered force 25 November 2005 — formal Banjul Charter Protocol on women's rights framework.

structural_findings: Banjul Charter operates a supranational African regional human-rights binding-treaty framework — places Banjul Charter in the Sub-Saharan African supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating formal combined individual + collective peoples' rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — Banjul Charter Article 19-24 peoples'-rights framework.

Third modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework is structurally distinctive globally — alongside ECHR + IACtHR frameworks.

Central jurisdiction of formal African Article 18 family-rights + Article 19-24 peoples'-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional human-rights binding-treaty framework operating formal combined family-rights + peoples'-rights framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child ACRWC 1990 complementary framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating formal complementary children's-rights binding-treaty framework.

55 of 55 African Union member state party ratification framework + Western Saharan Arab Democratic Republic SADR state party framework is structurally distinctive globally — universal African coverage framework.

Maputo Protocol 2003 + ACRWC 1990 + Banjul Charter 1981 + African Court Protocol 1998 + African Commission 1987 African-human-rights-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights + African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights formal dual-body framework is structurally distinctive globally.

sources.title: African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights
sources.publisher: African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights

sources.title: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
sources.publisher: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights

editorial_notes: Banjul Charter jurisdiction sidecar — supranational Sub-Saharan African regional human-rights binding-treaty meta-framework (African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights 1981/1986 + Article 18 Family + Articles 19-24 Peoples' Rights + Protocol Establishing the African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights 1998/2004 + ACRWC 1990/1999 + Maputo Protocol 2003/2005 + OAU Charter 1963 + AU 2002 + African Commission 1987). Only modern regional binding-treaty framework operating formal combined individual + collective peoples' rights framework globally + third modern regional binding-treaty framework operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework + central jurisdiction of formal African Article 18 family-rights + Article 19-24 peoples'-rights framework + central jurisdiction of formal African Charter on the Rights and Welfare of the Child ACRWC 1990 complementary framework + 55 of 55 African Union member state party ratification framework universal African coverage framework + Maputo Protocol 2003 + ACRWC 1990 + Banjul Charter 1981 + African Court Protocol 1998 + African Commission 1987 African-human-rights-framework-development sequence + African Court on Human and Peoples' Rights + African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights formal dual-body framework. African-Union regional triad complete (Banjul Charter 1981 + ACRWC 1990 + Maputo Protocol 2003).
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Sub-Saharan-African + supranational + regional-human-rights-binding-treaty cluster + Banjul-Charter-Article-18-family-and-Articles-19-24-peoples-rights-formal-combined-framework-globally-distinctive + African-Court-on-Human-and-Peoples-Rights-Protocol-1998-2004 + ACRWC-complementary-framework + Maputo-Protocol-2003-2005 + African-Commission-1987 + 55-of-55-AU-state-party-ratification + ECHR-IACtHR-African-Court-three-regional-courts clusters within the corpus.
